meals.label: Meal A
meals.name: Corn Chowder
ings: 1 tsp coarse salt, ½ tsp black pepper
1 Tbsp honey, ¼ tsp dried thyme, 2 bay leaves
steps: Cook bacon in heavy pot over medium until crispy. Remove, leaving ~3 Tbsp fat in the pot.
Add butter; cook onion and celery ~5 min. Add garlic 30 sec. Season with salt and pepper.
Sprinkle flour; cook 1–2 min. Pour in broth with potato, corn, honey, thyme, bay leaves. Bring to boil, simmer until potatoes tender, 10–12 min.
Discard bay leaves. Add cream. Blend to desired consistency with immersion blender (leave some chunks).
Stir in cheese until melted. Add most of bacon. Garnish bowls with remaining bacon and scallions.
meals.note: 💡 Naturally not spicy, super kid-friendly. Leave a bit chunky — kids love the corn kernels. Leftovers freeze well.

meals.label: Meal B
meals.name: One-Pan Coconut Curry Salmon
ings: 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp pepper, ½ tsp paprika
1 tsp curry powder, ½ tsp turmeric
Fresh cilantro or basil for garnish
steps: Pat salmon dry; season with salt, pepper, paprika. Heat oil in skillet over medium-high. Sear salmon skin-side down 3–4 min until crispy. Flip, cook 2 more min. Remove to a plate.
Lower heat; melt butter in same pan. Sauté garlic and ginger 1 min. Stir in curry powder and turmeric; toast 30 sec until fragrant.
Add coconut milk, tomato paste, honey, soy sauce, and lime juice. Whisk together and simmer 5 min until slightly thickened.
Return salmon to pan; spoon sauce over. Simmer 5 min until salmon is cooked through and sauce coats it.
Garnish with cilantro. Serve over jasmine rice with extra sauce spooned over the top.
meals.note: 💡 Skipping the red pepper flakes keeps this totally kid-friendly. The curry powder + turmeric give warmth and color without heat. Serve over rice to soak up the sauce.

meals.label: Meal C
meals.name: Homemade Crunchwrap Supreme
ings: 1 cup nacho cheese sauce (jarred is fine)
steps: Cook and crumble ground beef in a skillet over medium-high heat until no longer pink. Drain excess fat. Stir in taco seasoning per packet directions (usually add ⅔ cup water and simmer 3–5 min).
Warm nacho cheese in microwave (about 1 min). Warm tortillas in microwave 20 sec to make them pliable.
Lay a tortilla flat. In the center: spread 2 Tbsp nacho cheese, add ½ cup taco meat, place a tostada shell on top, spread thin layer of sour cream, add lettuce, tomato, and shredded cheese.
Fold tortilla: start with the bottom edge, fold it up to the center over the filling. Work around the circle, folding each section tight and overlapping to create a hexagonal seal.
Heat a lightly oiled skillet over medium. Cook the wrap seam-side down for 2–3 min until golden and sealed. Flip carefully and cook other side 2 min. Repeat for remaining wraps. Serve immediately.
meals.note: 💡 Kid-favorite assembly night. Use mild taco seasoning so the boys can have it as-is. Set out hot sauce on the side for adults. The seam-side-down sear is what keeps it together — don't skip.
